name: Canva Connect APIs
what_it_does: Enables AI agents to programmatically create, manage, and export Canva designs, apply brand templates, and manage digital assets at scale.
use_cases: Generate on-brand marketing materials by applying brand kit templates with dynamic content for each campaign or customer segment
Automate bulk creation of social media graphics from structured data (e.g., product catalog, event listings)
Export finalized designs as PDFs or images and upload them to downstream systems like CMS or DAM platforms
Manage and organize brand assets (logos, color palettes, fonts) across an organization's Canva team
Integrate design approval workflows by monitoring design status and triggering downstream actions on completion
not_for: Real-time pixel-level image manipulation or generative AI image editing (use dedicated image APIs instead)
Replacing a full-featured DAM system — Canva asset management is Canva-centric, not a general asset store
High-volume low-latency rendering pipelines — API is not optimized for sub-second bulk export throughput
best_when: An agent needs to produce polished, on-brand visual content at scale using pre-approved templates without requiring a human designer.
avoid_when: The task requires arbitrary raster/vector image manipulation, real-time rendering, or access to Canva features not yet exposed in the Connect API surface.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 with authorization code flow. Scopes are resource-granular (design:read, design:write, asset:read, asset:write, brandtemplate:read, profile:read). Requires Canva partner program enrollment for full API access including brand kit and bulk operations. Personal access tokens available for development.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Limited API calls during development; production access requires Canva partner approval. Free Canva accounts have restricted template and export capabilities.
pricing.paid_tiers: Canva for Teams or Enterprise subscription required for brand kit APIs and team management endpoints. Partner program agreement needed for production scale.
pricing.notes: Some API capabilities (brand templates, team asset management) are only available with Canva Teams or Enterprise plans. Partner program enrollment is required for production API keys beyond sandbox use.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits are enforced per OAuth app and vary by endpoint tier. Export and design creation endpoints have lower limits than read endpoints. Specific limits are communicated during partner onboarding; estimated 60–120 requests/minute for write operations.

requirements.min_contract: Partner program agreement required for production access

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include structured codes and messages, but some edge cases (export failures, template incompatibility) return generic errors with limited remediation guidance. Error surface is still maturing as the API is relatively new.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Design creation is not idempotent by default — repeated calls create duplicate designs. Agents must track created design IDs externally to avoid duplicates. Some read and export operations are safely idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Partner program enrollment is a manual process with approval delays — agents cannot self-serve production credentials, which blocks CI/CD automation pipelines until approval is granted
Export operations are asynchronous: agents must poll a job status endpoint rather than receiving an immediate download URL, requiring careful state management to avoid lost exports
Brand template population requires knowing exact placeholder names defined inside the template; there is no API to introspect placeholder schemas, so agents must rely on out-of-band metadata
OAuth access tokens are short-lived and refresh token rotation must be handled carefully — token expiry mid-workflow will silently fail design operations if the agent does not proactively refresh
Design duplication is not prevented by the API — agents that retry failed creation calls without checking for existing designs will accumulate duplicate copies in the user's account

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth 2.0 with granular scopes provides solid access control. Partner vetting adds a layer of trust before production credentials are issued. No mTLS or API-key-signing mechanism for elevated assurance. Webhook payloads should be verified using the provided HMAC signature header.
